html:
The Belt of Hunger is a wearable Magic item. It requires 6 Slurper Pelts, 2 Rope, and 2 Nightmare Fuel to craft and a Shadow Manipulator to prototype. The Sewing Kit repairs 63% of its durability.

While worn, it restores Sanity by 2/min and reduces Hunger loss by 40%.

Using the belt with characters that lose Hunger at a normal rate (75/day) will make them lose only 45 points per day. Wolfgang's normal form will lose 67.5 while his mighty form will lose 135/day, and Wes will lose 56.25/day.

Downloadable Content

In the Hamlet DLC, Belt of Hungers can be bought for 20 Oincs at The Flying Pig Arcane Shop in the Pig City, making it renewable in this DLC.

Tips

  • Because Slurpers do not respawn without regenerating the Caves with the Seed of Ruin, it's recommended to use the Sewing Kit on the Belt of Hunger to repair it rather than letting it degrade and crafting a new one.
  • A single use of the Sewing Kit restores enough durability to prevent 151 points worth of hunger drain, comparable to Meaty Stew. This makes sewing a Belt of Hunger an extremely efficient way prevent starvation.
  • This item is excellent for Wolfgang, as it completely nullifies his hunger problems and for Wigfrid, since her inability to eat non-Meaty foods can be very risky, especially when traveling to distant places from base.
    • It's also useful for Wes and Wilbur, due to practically nullifying their increased hunger drain (when running, for the latter).

Gallery

  • Wilson wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Willow wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Wolfgang wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Wendy wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • WX-78 wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Wickerbottom wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Woodie wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Wes wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Maxwell wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Wigfrid wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Webber wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Walani wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Warly wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Wilbur wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Woodlegs wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Wilba wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Wormwood wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Wheeler wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Winona wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Wortox wearing a Belt of Hunger.
  • Wurt wearing a Belt of Hunger.

Don't Starve
Together
Belt of Hunger
"Keep that belly full."
Object Info
Perk
Reduces hunger loss by 40%.
Restores sanity over time.
+2/minute
8 days
Repaired By Sewing
Does not stack
Source
( ×20)
Crafting
×6 ×2 ×2
Tab
Tier
Miscellaneous
"armorslurper"
“A soggy, sustaining, succulent suit.”
–Wilson
“Oh, ick! Ick! Ick! Ick! Eeeeeewwwwww!”
–Willow
“It moves with my many muscles!”
–Wolfgang
“A clinging, dreary, living suit.”
–Wendy
“FURRY INSULATION FOR MY WIRES.”
–WX-78
“A disgusting and magical garment.”
–Wickerbottom
“It tickles. I think it's still alive.”
–Woodie
“Wet, soggy, and oddly filling.”
–Maxwell
“This dark fuel could truly solve the world's hunger problems.”
–Wagstaff
“She ebbs the ache öf hunger. Gööd fur.”
–Wigfrid
“Squeeze our tummy tight!”
–Webber
“So efficient!”
–Walani
“Ah. My appetite wanes under its protection.”
–Warly
“How bad do I wanna protect myself?”
–Woodlegs
“MAKETH WILBA HAIRY AND SAFE”
–Wilba
“Furry belt. Looks good”
–Wormwood
“It'll be all the rage in Paris.”
–Wheeler
“So tight I barely remember the gnawing hunger!”
–Winona
“If it means I eat less often, count me in!”
–Wortox
“Good belt, glorp.”
–Wurt
“The smell kind of ruins my appetite.”
–Walter
“I guess I'll swallow my pride and wear it...”
–Wanda
The Belt of Hunger is a wearable Magic item. It requires 6 Slurper Pelts, 2 Rope, and 2 Nightmare Fuel to craft and a Shadow Manipulator to prototype. The Sewing Kit repairs 63% of its durability.
While worn, it restores Sanity by 2/min and reduces Hunger loss by 40%.
Using the belt with characters that lose Hunger at a normal rate (75/day) will make them lose only 45 points per day. Wolfgang's normal form will lose 67.5 while his mighty form will lose 135/day, and Wes will lose 56.25/day.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Hamlet DLC, Belt of Hungers can be bought for 20 Oincs at The Flying Pig Arcane Shop in the Pig City, making it renewable in this DLC.
Tips[edit | edit source]
Because Slurpers do not respawn without regenerating the Caves with the Seed of Ruin, it's recommended to use the Sewing Kit on the Belt of Hunger to repair it rather than letting it degrade and crafting a new one.
A single use of the Sewing Kit restores enough durability to prevent 151 points worth of hunger drain, comparable to Meaty Stew. This makes sewing a Belt of Hunger an extremely efficient way prevent starvation.
This item is excellent for Wolfgang, as it completely nullifies his hunger problems and for Wigfrid, since her inability to eat non-Meaty foods can be very risky, especially when traveling to distant places from base.
It's also useful for Wes and Wilbur, due to practically nullifying their increased hunger drain (when running, for the latter).

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Thắt lưng Đói là một vật phẩm Phép thuật có thể đeo được. Nó cần 6 Tấm Slurper, 2 Dây thừng và 2 Nhiên liệu Ác mộng để chế tạo và một Bộ điều khiển Bóng tối để tạo nguyên mẫu. Bộ May vá sửa chữa 63% độ bền của nó.

It requires 6 [[Slurper Pelt]]s, 2 [[Rope]], and 2 [[Nightmare Fuel]] to craft and a [[Shadow Manipulator]] to prototype. The [[Sewing Kit]] repairs 63% of its durability. While worn, it restores Sanity by 2/min and reduces [[Hunger]] loss by 40%. Using the belt with characters that lose Hunger at a normal rate (75/day) will make them lose only 45 points per day. [[Wolfgang|Wolfgang's]] normal form will lose 67.5 while his mighty form will lose 135/day, and [[Wes]] will lose 56.25/day. __NOTOC__ == {{Pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content == In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, Belt of Hungers can be bought for 20 [[Oinc]]s at [[The Flying Pig Arcane Shop]] in the [[Pig City]], making it renewable in this DLC. == {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * Because [[Slurper]]s do not respawn without regenerating the [[Caves]] with the [[Seed of Ruin]], it's recommended to use the Sewing Kit on the Belt of Hunger to repair it rather than letting it degrade and crafting a new one. * A single use of the Sewing Kit restores enough durability to prevent 151 points worth of hunger drain, comparable to [[Meaty Stew]]. This makes sewing a Belt of Hunger an extremely efficient way prevent starvation. * This item is excellent for Wolfgang, as it completely nullifies his hunger problems and for [[Wigfrid]], since her inability to eat [[Vegetables|non-Meaty foods]] can be very risky, especially when traveling to distant places from base. ** It's also useful for [[Wes]] and [[Wilbur]], due to practically nullifying their increased hunger drain (when running, for the latter). == {{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Belt of Hunger Wilson.png|[[Wilson]] wearing a Belt of Hunger.
